response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack/web engineer with strong TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang background, but the resume shows no native Japanese proficiency (a hard requirement), no embedded networking security experience, no Linux kernel modification work, and only one brief stint with C/C++ over a decade ago at National Instruments. The core technical and language requirements for this LAN-switch embedded security role are not met.
strengths: 15+ years of software development experience across multiple domains
Strong testing/TDD discipline and software craftsmanship mindset
Polyglot background including some low-level work with DLang and async I/O
concerns: No evidence of native-level Japanese language proficiency (hard requirement)
C experience is minimal and dated (~1 year at National Instruments, 2014–2015)
No demonstrated experience with embedded firmware, L2/L3 networking, MACsec/802.1X, or Linux kernel source modification

key_factors.factor: Lack of embedded networking/security domain experience
Insufficient C/UNIX and Linux kernel depth
